overview.tagline: A small, welcoming borough with tree-lined streets, friendly neighbors, and hometown charm
overview.description: Maywood is a compact, family-friendly borough in central Bergen County known for its tree-lined streets, affordable homes, and strong sense of community. With a population of about 10,000 in just over one square mile, Maywood offers a walkable, neighborly atmosphere where residents know each other and community events bring people together. The borough's central Bergen County location provides convenient access to major shopping, dining, and transportation, while its quiet residential streets offer a respite from the bustle of larger surrounding communities.
highlights: Compact, walkable borough with a small-town feel
Affordable homes relative to Bergen County averages
Active recreation department and community events
Central location near major shopping and highways
Strong community identity with engaged residents

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Maywood's housing stock is primarily mid-century single-family homes with some two-family and multi-family properties.
realEstate.recentTrends: Maywood has benefited from spillover demand as buyers priced out of more expensive Bergen County towns look for affordable options with good community character. Prices have appreciated steadily, and well-maintained homes sell quickly.
neighborhoodNotes: West Maywood — Quieter residential streets near Saddle Brook border
East Maywood — Near Hackensack and major shopping areas
Maywood Avenue area — Central location near schools and borough amenities
South end — Near Route 17 shopping and the Saddle River

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Maywood.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through the DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same schedule
Recycling bins available through the DPW
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is managed by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water New Jersey
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Quarterly water billing through Veolia
Water quality reports available online
Emergency repairs available through the DPW

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages on-street parking and regulations throughout the borough.
details: On-street parking available on most residential streets
No metered parking in the borough
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commuter parking available at nearby Hackensack bus terminal

additionalServices.title: Maywood Public Library
additionalServices.description: Public library serving borough residents with books, programs, and community space.
details: Located at 459 Maywood Avenue
Children's story times and summer reading programs
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreation programs for all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Senior citizen programs and trips
Community events and holiday celebrations
Athletic field and park programs

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Maywood enforces noise regulations to maintain the peaceful character of its residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Amplified music subject to enforcement after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours
Fences must comply with borough height requirements
Properties must be kept free of debris
Abandoned vehicles must be removed

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Maywood requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Nuisance barking subject to enforcement
No exotic or wild animals permitted

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough.
keyRules: Overnight parking restricted on certain borough streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles restricted on residential streets
No parking on lawns or unpaved surfaces
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties should be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability may be required
Landlords must maintain properties to code
Inspections at change of tenancy
Multi-family dwellings subject to additional fire codes

other.title: Fence Regulations
other.summary: Maywood regulates fence installation to maintain neighborhood character.
keyRules: Fences may not exceed 6 feet in rear yards
Front yard fences may not exceed 4 feet
Permit required for new fence installation
Chain-link fences subject to specific aesthetic requirements

publicSchools.name: Maywood Avenue Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Single elementary school serving the borough with a neighborhood school atmosphere

privateSchools.name: Our Lady Queen of Peace School

whyMoveHere: Affordable home prices relative to Bergen County averages
Small-town, neighborly atmosphere with strong community bonds
Central Bergen County location near major shopping and dining
Compact, walkable borough with tree-lined streets
Active recreation department with youth sports and community events
Close to Hackensack, Paramus, and Route 17 amenities

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus routes provide service from nearby Hackensack to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Residents can also drive to Secaucus Junction or other NJ Transit rail stations. The borough's central Bergen County location offers multiple commuting options.

costOfLiving.summary: Maywood offers an affordable cost of living for Bergen County, with housing prices below the county median and reasonable day-to-day expenses.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 10% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Friendly, close-knit community with active civic life
Central location with easy access to shopping and services
Quiet residential streets with a suburban small-town feel
Good value for Bergen County living
cons: No train station — reliant on bus or driving for NYC commute
Small borough with limited commercial offerings
School district is small with limited advanced program options
Older housing stock may require updates
Route 17 traffic can impact surrounding areas during peak hours

restaurants.name: Bonefish Grill
restaurants.description: Seafood-focused chain restaurant offering fresh fish, steaks, and cocktails in a polished casual setting.

restaurants.name: Seasons 52
restaurants.description: Fresh grill and wine bar offering seasonally inspired dishes with a focus on health-conscious dining.

restaurants.name: Bottagra
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian restaurant and wine bar offering refined Mediterranean cuisine in a stylish setting.

restaurants.name: Dinallo's Italian Restaurant
restaurants.description: Neighborhood Italian restaurant serving classic pasta, pizza, and Italian-American favorites.

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Borough's main park with playgrounds, athletic fields, basketball courts, and a walking path. Home to youth sports leagues and community events.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park (Maywood Section)
parks.description: Bergen County park along the Saddle River with walking and biking paths, connecting Maywood to neighboring communities.

parks.name: Maywood Community Park
parks.description: Small neighborhood park with a playground and sitting areas, convenient for families with young children.

events.name: Maywood Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair with vendors, food, rides, and live entertainment, bringing the community together.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade and ceremony honoring veterans, organized by local civic organizations.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, carols, and community gathering.

attractions.name: Saddle River County Park
attractions.description: Scenic county park with a multi-use trail system running through Maywood along the Saddle River, ideal for walking, jogging, and cycling.

attractions.name: Garden State Plaza
attractions.description: One of New Jersey's premier shopping malls in nearby Paramus, with hundreds of stores, restaurants, and entertainment options.

shoppingAreas: Maywood Avenue — Local shops and services
Route 17 — Major retail corridor minutes away

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 459 Maywood Avenue, Maywood, NJ 07607

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
